item.title: So stinking glad I'm out of corporate America. Self-employment is SO MUCH BETTER
item.description:
Absolutely detest corporate double speak buzzword nonsense
Take them for every penny they've got
Come over here and get your free virus!
I mean I guess I still do this
You weren't supposed to see that
At a certain age you just start to really appreciate all the up and coming talent
No way. You're not going to interrupt my lunch
Luuuuuunch
Did I mention I hate corporate buzzword nonsense double speak?
It's someone else's problem now
Learn to stand up for yourselves people
...Barf
Yummy! stale sandwiches

item.title: Who's up for a preposterously long thread about why you don't know jack about Russia? Everyone? Neat.
item.description:
Sorry I said you don't know jack. I'm sure you know some things. I knew some of these things, but the picture they paint put together is dire for Russia, and it pretty much killed any hope I had of someone staging a military coup to get them out of the mess Putin has made for them. To find out why read on, and fair warning, it's *really* long. Maybe just favourite and forget right now. No-one will judge. (Oh, and pretty sure English isn't Kamil's first language so cut him some slack, word around the campfire is your Russian isn't so hot either, pal.)
How's it going? So proud of you for making it this far. You're half way!
Ok so I lied a bit before, you were halfway through *this* post. I've actually run out of juice because the screenshot app I use can't go any further than this. To be fair this screenshot was almost 25000 pixels tall (split in half for imgur). If you've come this far and still want to read the rest there's about 30 more tweets starting here. https://twitter.com/kamilkazani/status/1502733326330773508
item.dc:creator: TheGhostofElizabethShue

item.title: Boycott Intuit
item.description: It really sucks that for many years Intuit has lobbied the government so that filing taxes is a chore that costs money. Using TurboTax funds these lobbyists. It's not that our government can't calculate how much we owe them. Its that corporations like Intuit want to force us to buy their products. If you use turbotax, maybe try to torrent it. Edit: Sorry people, I meant for USA, idk about other countries. Edit 2 (Electric Bugaloo): I never thought I'd finally have a post with so many upbotes. Thanks all for participating in the discussion! Edit 3: Ok maybe dont torrent it because that can be sketch. Point is ONE SHOULDNT HAVE TO PAY TO PAY TAXES
item.dc:creator: Remishnok

item.description: This will probably die in usersub.. I just don't think I can take feeling this way anymore. Doctors, three med changes, tharpists. Please somebody make this pain go away.

item.title: Day 295: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Shack Stack Beef Patty, Crisp-fried Portobello Mushroom Filled With Melted Muenster And Cheddar Cheeses, Topped With Lettuce, Tomato, Shacksauce From Shake Shack Multiple Locations Across The Globe Previous Day 294: https://imgur.com/gallery/3XmpBBc MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD
item.dc:creator: crypto

item.title: Fuck Budget
item.description:
Finally moving out of trash apartment complex to a new state. Plan was to take some of our stuff with us and put it in storage near where we’re moving, do some interviews and apply for apartments. When we came out last night to start the drive, the full truck was gone.
We drive to the tow yard that’s posted at our complex they don’t have it. Call the non emergency police, they track it down to Budget towing it cuz the complex told them to. After hours of getting the ring around we find where it was dropped off only for the people there to say it’s not there anymore. At this point I’ve had to reschedule an important job interview, on the verge of a panic attack, and I don’t think my partner has ever been this angry.
We go there in person and it is there! The mechanic had moved it without telling them and they couldn’t see it from their office. After getting the whole story, they say we can go and don’t have to pay any towing fees! Only to find out that the truck won’t start because said mechanic “took something out so they can’t just drive away without paying tow fees”. Except 1) no one told him to do this 2) we don’t have to pay, we have permission from 3 people above him and 3) mechanic won’t be in until Monday.
They finally get ahold of the mechanic and verbally tear him to shreds and make him walk through another employee how to fix the truck. Ten minutes later we drive off with no towing fees and an extra two days to our rental at no cost to us! **Most Viral Edit: I’d like to thank the academy and viewers just like you**
item.dc:creator: Technicolorluver

item.title: It's called Sealioning
item.description: https://www.comicsands.com/trolls-sealioning-scientists-social-media-2631216981.htmlSocial media like Imgur is a potentially lucrative market - there are 300 MILLION users on Imgur. Needless to say, it's a pretty good place to advertise products, be it an aspiring author with a book to pitch, or a film studio seeking to drum up interest in their upcoming release. It's also an opportunity to steer public opinion in one direction or another. To manufacture consent for another war, to divert attention away from a politician's public scandal, to downplay negative reviews of an overhyped product. So be advised that some of the people on Imgur are being less than honest in their opinions. Some of the most common tactics used: Ad hominem attacks (obviously). Reliance on captioned images rather than actual responses. Sealioning. That last one bears a bit of explanation, and I'll let this guy Marshall Shepherd do the explaining: https://www.forbes.com/sites/marshallshepherd/2019/03/07/sealioning-is-a-common-trolling-tactic-7ea327f67a41 edit: Forbes appears to be grumpy about letting people link to it from Imgur. Here's a few other links: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sealioning And: https://www.comicsands.com/trolls-sealioning-scientists-social-media-2631216981.html And: http://wondermark.com/1k62/ So if you're dealing with someone behaving like this - whether the topic is on politics, or climate change, or anything else - they're probably not arguing in good faith. Just stop responding to them. Save your opinions for someone who, even if they disagree with you, will debate you more honestly. Edit: thanks to mormonbatman for informing me that the description for the overarching strategy is "astroturfing," and "sealioning" is one of the methods employed: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astroturfing
item.dc:creator: ArkoneAxon

item.title: One of the most unique, special and elusive species on earth The Narwhal
item.description: One of the most unique, special and elusive species on earth The Narwhal The narwhal looks like a cross between a whale and a unicorn with its long, spiraled tusk jutting from its head. Males most commonly have tusks, and some may even have two. The tusk, which can grow as long as 10 feet, is actually an enlarged tooth. Ongoing research by WWF collaborators indicates that the tusk has sensory capability, with up to 10 million nerve endings inside. The tusk may also play a role in the ways males exert dominance. Narwhals spend their lives in the Arctic waters of Canada, Greenland, Norway and Russia. The majority of the world’s narwhals winter for up to five months under the sea ice in the Baffin Bay-Davis Strait area (between Canada and western Greenland). Cracks in the ice allow them to breathe when needed, especially after dives, which can be up to a mile and a half deep. They feed mainly on Greenland halibut, along with other fish, squid and shrimp...
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007
